title: CC narrative review S16b panel a1

payload.notes:
Beat B09.5 delivered fully: star-map-as-sky with the night's path idea->REJECT->WARN_corrected->ACCEPT->paper, all 4 expected literals baked large, DDL T-02:41 = before dawn, and both bubbles verbatim ("连被拒的那条也在。" / "失败也是记忆。"). NARRATIVE-ONLY judgment of panels/S16b_panel_a01.png.

BEAT FIDELITY 5/5 — This is the memory beat: the whole night cools into a constellation and the research-wiki thesis is delivered literally, not narrated. The four gated labels are the arc's audit trail re-encountered as stars (REJECT from S11, WARN_corrected from S15, ACCEPT from S16), the paper star burns brightest at the top right, and the unlabeled ember side-stars hang dim under idea — the dead novelty branches present but not lit, exactly the 'failure is archived as memory' point. The executor's hand hovers over the dim red REJECT star without touching it while the reviewer's 「失败也是记忆。」 lands the skill's thesis in five characters; the duo standing SMALL at the table edge under the open sky reads as settled (not deflated), the correct emotional register after S16's earned ACCEPT. DDL T-02:41 keeps the deadline thread alive even while the panel exhales — before dawn, with time still running.

COMPOSITION (STORY) 4/5 — Reading order works: RESEARCH WIKI header → the path lighting node by node left-to-right → the rise into ACCEPT and paper → the duo and bubbles grounding the foreground. Docked one point: the storyboard's conceit is TABLE-IS-THE-SKY (one map), but the render shows the path twice — large in the sky and a smaller duplicate trace with its own mini REJECT/WARN_corrected/ACCEPT glyphs on the holographic table surface — which splits the metaphor and the eye between two copies; the two speech bubbles also sit right on the sky/table seam, cropping the lower trace. Neither hurts beat legibility, but the single-image-as-sky idea reads ~90% rather than 100%. Visual identity-lock / artifact severity / baked-text legibility / content-corruption are NOT my call — left to the visual reviewers (Gemini/Codex).
